body.thing: code_indexer shell sweep is read-only over the source tree
body.intention: Indexing a .sh file records its intent in clan.code_index but never modifies the source file's bytes

red_run.summary:
mock_cursor.fetchone.return_value = None  # no existing row
    
        with patch("devlab.claudecode.code_indexer._haiku_intent", return_value="test script echoes test"), \
             patch("psycopg2.connect", return_value=mock_conn):
            result = sweep_shell_files(repo, db_url="postgresql://test/db")
    
        # The DB write still happens (intent recorded in clan.code_index) ...
        assert result["inserted"] == 1
        assert result["errors"] == 0
        # ... but the source file is byte-for-byte unchanged — no in-band mutation.

body.why: A reindex silently editing tracked source produced dirty-tree surprises right after a clean commit/proof (CP6: no surprise mutation).
